query: CDC RSV vaccine recommendations 2025 adults 75 older 50 74 high risk single dose repeat dosing

mako.raw:
{"query":"CDC RSV vaccine recommendations 2025 adults 75 older 50 74 high risk single dose repeat dosing","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1761,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.cdc.gov/rsv/hcp/vaccine-clinical-guidance/adults.html","title":"RSV Vaccine Guidance for Adults | RSV | CDC","content":"CDC recommends a single dose of respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) vaccine for all adults ages 75 and older and adults ages 50–74 at increased risk of severe RSV illness.
There are three FDA-licensed RSV vaccines recommended for use in adults ages 50 and older: GSK’s Arexvy, Moderna’s mResvia, and Pfizer’s Abrysvo.
CDC does not have a preferential recommendation for any specific vaccine.
Surveillance and evaluation activities are ongoing to determine whether adults might benefit from receiving additional RSV vaccine doses in the future.
Refer to the Pfizer Abrysvo FDA package insert, GSK Arexvy FDA package insert, Moderna mResvia FDA package insert, and CDC RSV Vaccine Information Sheet for contraindications, precautions, and additional safety information.
In addition, effectiveness was demonstrated among adults ages 60 years and older with certain immunocompromising conditions and those with end-stage renal disease and in all adults ages 75 years and older.
In this trial, efficacy of a single dose of mResvia against symptomatic RSV was approximately 80% during the first 4 months following vaccination, 56% during the first 12 months after vaccination, and 34% through 18 months after vaccination."},{"url":"https://www.cdc.gov/rsv/vaccines/adults.html","title":"Vaccines for Adults | RSV | CDC","content":"CDC will evaluate how long protection lasts and may update recommendations, as needed."},{"url":"https://www.cdc.gov/rsvvaxview/dashboard/adults-50-coverage-intent.html","title":"Respiratory Syncytial Virus (RSV) Vaccination Coverage and Intent for Vaccination, Adults 75 Years and Older and Adults 50–74 Years with High-Risk Conditions*, United States | RSVVaxView | CDC","content":"# Respiratory Syncytial Virus (RSV) Vaccination Coverage and Intent for Vaccination, Adults 75 Years and Older and Adults 50–74 Years with High-Risk Conditions*, United States
Weekly estimates of RSV vaccination coverage and intent for vaccination among adults 50 years and older are calculated using data from the National Immunization Surveys (NIS)-Fall Respiratory Virus Module (NIS-FRVM).
In June 2025, the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices (ACIP) voted to recommend that adults aged 50–74 years who are at increased risk for severe RSV disease may receive a single dose of an RSV vaccine.
Interviews conducted since October 1, 2025, were included."},{"url":"https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12791291/","title":"P-1487. Respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) vaccination in adults aged <60 years at increased risk of severe RSV disease: considerations of the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention Advisor","content":"Respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) vaccination in adults aged <60 years at increased risk of severe RSV disease: considerations of the U.S.
Collection date 2026 Jan.
ACIP members discussed variability in individual-level risk of severe RSV disease in persons with specific chronic conditions and the balance of benefits and risks of RSV vaccination in adults aged < 60 years, who generally experience lower risk of severe outcomes from RSV infection compared with older adults.
Overall, Committee members thought that the public health benefits of RSV vaccination outweighed risks for adults aged 50–59 years with certain chronic medical conditions.
On April 16, 2025, ACIP recommended a single dose of RSV vaccine for these adults using the same qualifying risk conditions used for adults aged 60–74 years."},{"url":"https://www.aha.org/news/headline/2024-08-07-cdc-updates-rsv-vaccine-recommendations-older-adults","title":"CDC updates RSV vaccine recommendations for older adults | AHA News","content":"The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention has updated respiratory syncytial virus vaccination recommendations for adults 60 and older.
The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention July 14 released an advisory on the growing cyclosporiasis cases across the U.S."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"CDC RSV vaccine recommendations 2025 adults 75 older 50 74 high risk single dose repeat dosing","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.cdc.gov/rsv/hcp/vaccine-clinical-guidance/adults.html","title":"RSV Vaccine Guidance for Adults","content":"*   CDC recommends a single dose of respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) vaccine for all adults ages 75 and older and adults ages 50–74 at increased risk of severe RSV illness. *   There are three FDA-licensed RSV vaccines recommended for use in adults ages 50 and older: GSK’s Arexvy, Moderna’s mResvia, and Pfizer’s Abrysvo. *   Other chronic medical conditions or risk factors that a healthcare provider determines would increase the risk for severe disease due to viral respiratory infection (e.g., frailty,C situations in which healthcare providers have concern for presence of undiagnosed chronic medical conditions, or residence in a remote or rural community where transportation of patients with severe RSV disease for escalation of medical care is challengingD). **The RSV vaccine is not currently an annual vaccine, meaning eligible adults do not need to get a dose every RSV season.** Currently, CDC recommends only a single dose of RSV vaccine for all adults ages 75 and older and for adults ages 50–74 with increased risk of severe RSV illness.","score":0.90039873,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://arexvyhcp.com/practice-education/cdc-recommendation","title":"CDC RSV Vaccine Recommendation | AREXVY (Respiratory Syncytial Virus Vaccine, Adjuvanted)","content":"The site you are linking to is not controlled or endorsed by GSK, and GSK is not responsible for the content provided on that site. # The CDC RSV Vaccine Recommendation. * Adults 50-74 years of age who are at increased risk of severe RSV illness receive a single dose of RSV vaccine. * Adults 75 years of age and older receive a single dose of RSV vaccine. Eligible adults are currently recommended to receive a single dose of RSV vaccine; adults who have already received RSV vaccination should not receive another dose. Patient attestation is sufficient evidence of the presence of a risk factor; vaccinators should not deny RSV vaccination to a person because of lack of documentation.1,2. #### Data on the safety and efficacy of AREXVY in individuals with these risk factors for severe RSV disease as described by the CDC may be limited.3. AREXVY is a vaccine indicated for active immunization for the prevention of lower respiratory tract disease (LRTD) caused by respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) in:.","score":0.87166095,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.cdc.gov/rsvvaxview/dashboard/adults-50-coverage-intent.html","title":"Respiratory Syncytial Virus (RSV) Vaccination Coverage and Intent for Vaccination, Adults 75 Years and Older and Adults 50–74 Years with High-Risk Conditions*, United States","content":"# Respiratory Syncytial Virus (RSV) Vaccination Coverage and Intent for Vaccination, Adults 75 Years and Older and Adults 50–74 Years with High-Risk Conditions*, United States. Weekly estimates of RSV vaccination coverage and intent for vaccination are calculated using data from the National Immunization Survey-Fall Respiratory Virus Module (NIS-FRVM). ## Adults 50+ RSV Vaccination Coverage and Intent. Weekly estimates of RSV vaccination coverage and intent for vaccination among adults 50 years and older are calculated using data from the National Immunization Surveys (NIS)-Fall Respiratory Virus Module (NIS-FRVM). *   **Figure 1A.** Cumulative Percentage of Adults 75 Years and Older and Adults 50–74 Years with High-Risk Conditions Ever Vaccinated with RSV Vaccine, 2025─26*,†,‡,§,^. *   **Figure 1B.** Weekly Intent± for Vaccination and Cumulative Percentage of Adults 75 Years and Older and Adults 50–74 Years with High-Risk Conditions Ever Vaccinated with RSV Vaccine, 2025–26*,†,‡,§,^. *   **Figure 1C.** Differences in Cumulative Coverage of Ever Receiving a Dose of RSV Vaccine by Selected Demographics, Adults 75 Years and Older and Adults 50–74 Years with High-Risk Conditions, United States*,†,‡,§,^.","score":0.8332299,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.cdc.gov/rsv/vaccines/adults.html","title":"Vaccines for Adults | RSV","content":"A **.gov** website belongs to an official government organization in the United States. A **lock** (  ) or **https://** means you've safely connected to the .gov website. *   CDC recommends adults ages 50–74 who are at increased risk of severe RSV illness get an RSV vaccine. If you have already received an RSV vaccine (including last year), you should not get another RSV vaccine at this time. *   You can get an RSV vaccine at any time of the year, but the best time to get vaccinated is in late summer and early fall. These vaccines work by causing an immune response that can protect you from severe respiratory disease if you get RSV in the future. CDC recommends an RSV vaccine for **all adults ages 75 years and older** and for **adults ages 50–74 years****who are at increased risk of severe RSV illness.**. This means that if you have received an RSV vaccine already, you should not get another RSV vaccine at this time.","score":0.81979275,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.vaccineadvisor.com/news/cdc-now-recommends-rsv-vaccine-for-some-adults-50-years-and-older","title":"CDC Now Recommends RSV Vaccine for Some Adults 50 Years and Older","content":"# CDC Now Recommends RSV Vaccine for Some Adults 50 Years and Older. HealthDay News — Adults as young as 50 may now qualify for an RSV vaccine if they have certain health conditions, according to a quiet update from the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC). This marks a change from the CDC’s previous recommendation, which only offered the shot to people 60 and older who were at high risk, the *Associated Press* reported. Earlier this year, a group of vaccine experts on the CDC’s Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices (ACIP) voted to expand RSV vaccination to high-risk adults starting at age 50. The site now states that vaccinating high-risk adults 50 and older is an “official recommendation of the CDC,” the *AP* added. The CDC already recommends the RSV shot for all adults 75 and older and for people 60 and older who have health conditions that increase their risk of severe RSV.","score":0.81418616,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.82,"request_id":"6e18a601-e838-4f82-b98d-713e9a4c2e6d"}
